ground_truth.title: Best Books Recommended by Newsroom Reporters | Books | theadvocate.com
ground_truth.author: Advocate Staff
ground_truth.main_content: We asked the newsroom to pick their favorite books of the year. Here are 6 recommendations. From a 35-year-old novel that still feels fresh to a sweeping history of Christianity, our reporters share what they have been reading. For those who like their fiction with historical context, 'The Eight' by Katherine Neville is a thriller set in two time periods. The book alternates between 1970s Georgetown and 1790s revolutionary France, centering on a chess game between grandmasters. MacCulloch's 'Christianity: The First Three Thousand Years' examines the faith's long history. Douglas Adams' 'The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy' remains funny after all these years. Larry McMurtry's 'Lonesome Dove' tells the story of retired Texas Rangers on a cattle drive. Denis Johnson's 'Tree of Smoke' is a sprawling novel about the Vietnam War. Isaac Asimov's 'I, Robot' collects short stories about artificial intelligence.
